ksa_text: Skill in managing cross-functional data and AI programs, including workstream coordination, dependency tracking, stakeholder communication, and escalation management.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of information technology (IT) acquisition/procurement requirements.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Operations & Program Management is a primary or secondary domain for the Director of Analytics. This skill KSA is within scope for the role's expected competency profile and seniority level (director).

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope (Operations & Program Management), seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with inclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Pass 1 signal is moderate (composite 0.506). External reviewers may question whether this specific skill KSA is sufficiently distinctive for the Director of Analytics versus adjacent roles.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: PMI, TOGAF operational frameworks, and CDO survey data (NewVantage Partners) confirm operational program management as a key competency for data leaders.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: moderate framework signal is consistent with Operations & Program Management appearing in job postings and role definition literature for Director of Analytics.

decision_rationale: Operations & Program Management skill KSA is within scope for the Director of Analytics at the director tier. Seniority fit is appropriate. All three evaluation passes support inclusion.
